asyncAnsweringQuestions: Answering student questions (based on your profile).

background.description: Let's get to know you better! Please fill out your background information so others in the MindHive community can connect with you more easily.

officialBio.description: In ~100 words, describe your expertise, experience, and educational background for a high school audience.
officialBio.title: Official Bio

background.title: Background

unofficialBio.description: In a couple of sentences, describe your journey leading to where you are now, professionally. And look ahead to the next 5 years or beyond. The goal is for students to understand that professional lives are not always a straight path and that there are coincidences, good luck and bad luck, and sometimes multiple career paths hidden behind official bios you might read on a scientist's website.
unofficialBio.title: Unofficial Bio

basic.description: Please complete the following items to create your profile and connect with the MindHive community.
basic.subtitle: Your basic information helps the MindHive community get to know you better. This includes who you are, where you're located, and what you look like.
basic.title: Basic Information

connectSubtitle: Search for, discover, and connect with the MindHive community

organization.intro1: To ensure a successful capstone experience, each sponsoring organization must designate a primary sponsor who will serve as a stakeholder and point of contact for the student team. The designated sponsor will be the key liaison for all project-related communications.
organization.intro2: Capstone projects are most successful when sponsors provide high-level strategic guidance while mentors offer day-to-day insights and support. Your involvement is essential to both student learning and project success.
organization.leadAvailabilityDescription: Please estimate a numerical time per week or per month. Refrain from answering "meet when available" or similar response.
organization.title: Point of Contact

description.individual: Please complete the following items to create your profile and connect with the MindHive community.
description.organization: Please complete the following items to create your organization's profile and connect with the MindHive community.

organizationFields.locationDescription: Please list the city and state/country where you'll be operating from.
organizationFields.missionDescription: Please describe your organization or unit including mission, key program activities, and nature of work.

sponsorMentor.additionalNotes: Is there any additional information you would like to highlight?
sponsorMentor.backgroundMethodologyDescription: Please include any important contextual information related to your project, and outline your methodologies including specific research approaches, data collection techniques, tools, and technologies.
sponsorMentor.categorizationDescription: Which category does your project align with?
sponsorMentor.competenciesDescription: List any specific skills or qualifications that would be useful for students to have in order to successfully address this problem and complete the capstone project. For example, user experience design, software programming, economics/financial analysis, public policy analysis, quantitative research/analysis, engineering skills (mechanical, electrical, etc). Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.dataRequirements: What are the anticipated data requirements for the project, and how will each required data resource be provided to the capstone team?
sponsorMentor.dataRequirementsDescription: Please describe what datasets will be used. For example, existing source data will be provided by the sponsor, existing source data is free and open (public), etc. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.dataSecurityConcerns: Does the sponsor have any anticipated data security concerns to be cleared for student team?
sponsorMentor.dataSecurityNotes: If yes or maybe, please describe concerns.
sponsorMentor.fieldWorkLikelihood: How likely would the project require field work or site visits (either within NYC or outside)?
sponsorMentor.guidelinesDescription: I have read and understood the Capstone proposal guidelines in full, including all of the Capstone Sponsor FAQs and Mutual Expectations agreement and agree to abide by them.
sponsorMentor.guidelinesRequestAppointment: I request an appointment to discuss further.
sponsorMentor.intro1: Sponsors who wish to take on a more hands-on role in guiding students can indicate their interest by selecting the checkbox below. By doing so, they acknowledge their understanding of the mentor's responsibilities and time commitment. As a mentor, sponsors will provide students with regular feedback, subject matter expertise, and practical insights to help them navigate project challenges and refine their work.
sponsorMentor.intro2: This role requires active engagement throughout the capstone experience, ensuring students receive the support needed to produce meaningful outcomes. If selected, sponsors will serve as both the primary stakeholder and a mentor, bridging strategic direction with day-to-day guidance.
sponsorMentor.learningOutcomesDescription: Provide 2-3 learning outcomes or expected deliverables. For example, an interactive dashboard or a website showcasing data analysis outcomes. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.projectAbstractDescription: 100 words maximum. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.projectDescriptionDescription: 250 words maximum. Describe the scope and nature of the capstone project. Please ensure that the project is achievable within the academic timeframe and with the resources available. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
sponsorMentor.projectTitleDescription: 15 words maximum. Tips: Be engaging for student appeal
sponsorMentor.relevanceDescription: Describe why this issue is of relevance to CUSP's mission and urban science.
sponsorMentor.relevantLinksDescription: This can include a project website, GitHub repository, or other online presence where one can learn more about the project. We'll include QR codes in the brochure.
sponsorMentor.researchQuestion: What is the research question/scope to be explored OR what is the problem that you want to address?
sponsorMentor.sponsorIsMentor: I understand the time commitment and intend to serve as the sponsor AND mentor.
sponsorMentor.techRequirements: Does the sponsor have technology infrastructure and/or software application requirements for this project?
sponsorMentor.techRequirementsDescription: For example, the project requires GIS data analysis and the sponsor uses ArcGIS or RShiny for mapping. If the sponsor has no preferences as to data tools used, please indicate.
sponsorMentor.title: Sponsor & Mentor

description.individual: What topics would you like to support students with? Select some topics and areas of interest from the list below to let other community members know where you're excited to contribute. This will help us connect you with students and peers who share your passions.
description.organization: What topics would you like your organization to support students with? Select some topics and areas of interest from the list below to let other community members know where you're excited to contribute. This will help us connect you with students and peers who share your passions.

customQuestions.description: Optional questions students answer when they rank this opportunity. Used by the matching algorithm.

customQuestions.saveFirst: Save the opportunity first, then come back here to add custom questions.

opportunityEditor.introVideoHint: Upload a video file or paste an embed URL (YouTube, Vimeo). Uploaded files are stored on our cloud bucket.
opportunityEditor.introVideoUrlHint: Or paste a video URL (the URL itself — not the full <iframe> embed code). Supports YouTube, Vimeo, Loom, Google Drive, or a direct .mp4/.webm link. Maximum upload size: {{maxMb}} MB.

Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.
opportunityEditor.mentorNotesDescription: Name, title, contact, and availability of the day-to-day mentor. If no mentor is available, describe the gaps and CUSP can help identify support.
opportunityEditor.offeredInNetworksHint: Pick which networks can see this opportunity.
opportunityEditor.projectAbstractDescription: 100 words maximum. Info provided here may be used on the CUSP website, in other promotional materials, and in Urban Science Intensive course descriptions for students.

Please ensure that the project is achievable within the academic timeframe and with the resources available. opportunityEditor.relevantLinksDescription: This can include a project website, GitHub repository, or other online presence where one can learn more about the project. We'll include QR codes in the brochure.
opportunityEditor.researchQuestion: What is the research question/scope to be explored OR what is the problem that you want to address?
opportunityEditor.sponsorIsMentor: I'm also the day-to-day mentor for this project (uncheck if someone else will be mentoring)
opportunityEditor.teamPreferences: Allow students to nominate preferred teammates for this opportunity
opportunityEditor.techRequirements: Does the sponsor have technology infrastructure and/or software application requirements for this project?

opportunityEditor.timeCommitmentPlaceholder: e.g. 3 hours per week for 8 weeks

validation.guidelines: Please tick the guidelines acknowledgment in the Publishing card before publishing this opportunity.
validation.projectDescription: Capstone Project Description & Overview is required.
validation.relevantLinks: At least one relevant link with a URL is required.
validation.researchQuestion: Research question / problem to address is required.
validation.title: Title is required.

interestSelector.placeholder: Begin typing to search for interests

introduction.description: We'd love for you to upload an introduction video to share with students and fellow MindHive members. You can use it to share a project you think students might get excited about.

passion.description: For example, you can briefly describe a science project you are working on. Or you can tell us about your experience bringing science to the public. This is your space to showcase what you're currently working on and how it shapes the expertise you bring to our community.
passion.title: What are you passionate about right now?
